exercises.title: Hypertree Parameter Calculation
exercises.description: Calculate hypertree parameters and signature sizes for different configurations.
tasks: For h=64, d=8: calculate XMSS tree height and total leaf count
For h=66, d=22: calculate the same values
Compare signature sizes for both configurations (assume n=16, w=16, k=33, a=6)
Calculate the probability of path collision after 2^30 signatures for each
Explain the trade-off between d (layers) and signature size
exercises.expectedOutput: Parameter analysis with signature size calculations
hints: More layers = more WOTS+ signatures in each signature
Collision probability after q signatures ≈ q²/2^h

exercises.title: FORS Security Analysis
exercises.description: Analyze FORS security under different usage patterns.
tasks: Calculate the probability of forgery after 1 signature with k=33, a=6
Calculate forgery probability after 100 signatures
Calculate forgery probability after 2^20 signatures
Determine the maximum recommended signatures per FORS key
Compare with one-time signature (OTS) security degradation
hints: After q signatures, approximately q×k leaves are revealed
Forgery requires all k indices to be among revealed leaves
OTS: security completely fails after 2 signatures

exercises.title: Hypertree Path Implementation
exercises.description: Implement hypertree path computation and verification.
tasks: Implement index computation from message digest
Compute layer-by-layer tree and leaf indices
Simulate signature path through all layers
Implement path verification logic
Test with multiple messages and verify consistency
exercises.starterCode:
import hashlib
from typing import List, Tuple

class HypertreePath:
    def __init__(self, h: int = 64, d: int = 8, n: int = 32):
        self.h = h
        self.d = d
        self.h_prime = h // d
        self.n = n
    
    def compute_path_indices(self, message: bytes, 
                             randomizer: bytes) -> List[Tuple[int, int]]:
        """
        Compute (tree_index, leaf_index) for each layer.
        
        Returns list of d tuples, one per layer from bottom to top.
        """
        # Hash to get digest
        digest = hashlib.sha256(randomizer + message).digest()
        
        # Extract initial tree and leaf index
        # tree_bits = h - h' bits, leaf_bits = h' bits
        tree_bits = self.h - self.h_prime
        
        idx_tree = int.from_bytes(digest[:8], 'big') % (2 ** tree_bits)
        idx_leaf = int.from_bytes(digest[8:16], 'big') % (2 ** self.h_prime)
        
        # Your implementation: compute indices for each layer
        path = []
        
        # TODO: Fill in path computation
        
        return path
    
    def verify_path_consistency(self, path: List[Tuple[int, int]]) -> bool:
        """
        Verify that the path indices are consistent.
        Each layer's leaf index should match the tree index encoding.
        """
        # Your implementation
        pass

# Test
ht = HypertreePath(h=24, d=4, n=16)  # Smaller for testing

message = b"Test message"
randomizer = b"random_16_bytes!"

path = ht.compute_path_indices(message, randomizer)
print(f"Path through hypertree (h={ht.h}, d={ht.d}):")
for layer, (tree, leaf) in enumerate(path):
    print(f"  Layer {layer}: tree={tree}, leaf={leaf}")
exercises.expectedOutput: Working hypertree path computation with verification
hints: Bottom layer: use full tree index and leaf index
Upper layers: combine tree and leaf from below
Tree index in layer i determines which tree in layer i+1
Leaf index determines which WOTS+ key is used

exercises.title: Signature Size Optimization
exercises.description: Analyze how different parameter choices affect signature size.
tasks: List all components of a SPHINCS+ signature
Calculate size of each component for SLH-DSA-128f
Calculate size of each component for SLH-DSA-128s
Identify which component contributes most to size
Propose a hypothetical parameter set optimized for your use case
exercises.expectedOutput: Detailed size breakdown and optimization analysis
hints: WOTS+: len × n bytes per signature
Auth paths: h' × n bytes per layer
